content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	#
3	# vendor-fetch.sh -- materialize vendored toolchain binaries from
4	# the manifests committed under vendor/<tool>/<version>/version.json.
5	#
6	# Two modes:
7	#
8	#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh                  -- verify mode (default)
9	#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --record <tool>  -- first-time pin a SHA-256
10	#
11	# Verify mode is what runs on every fresh clone or after pulling
12	# new commits. For each tool listed in vendor/active.env, it makes
13	# sure the binary at vendor/<tool>/<version>/bin/<tool> exists and
14	# matches the SHA-256 in version.json. If the binary is missing or
15	# stale, it rebuilds at the recorded commit and re-verifies.
16	#
17	# Record mode is for first-time vendoring of a new tool/version,
18	# before any SHA-256 has been pinned. It rebuilds at the recorded
19	# commit, then *writes* the resulting SHA-256 into version.json.
20	#
21	# Critical guarantee: builds always happen at the commit recorded
22	# in version.json, never at upstream HEAD. This is what makes
23	# rollback and controlled adoption possible.
24	#
25	# Dependencies: bash 4+, jq, git, sha256sum (or shasum -a 256).
26	#
27	# See docs/vendor-plan.md for the broader design.
28	
29	set -euo pipefail
30	
31	# --- Locate ourselves and the repo root --------------------------------------
32	
33	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
34	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
35	VENDOR_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/vendor"
36	ACTIVE_ENV="$VENDOR_DIR/active.env"
37	
38	cd "$REPO_ROOT"
39	
40	# --- Argument parsing --------------------------------------------------------
41	
42	MODE="verify"
43	RECORD_TOOL=""
44	FORCE_RECORD=0
45	
46	while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
47	    case "$1" in
48	        --record)
49	            MODE="record"
50	            RECORD_TOOL="${2:-}"
51	            if [ -z "$RECORD_TOOL" ]; then
52	                echo "error: --record requires a tool name (e.g. --record sw-cx24)" >&2
53	                exit 2
54	            fi
55	            shift 2
56	            ;;
57	        --force)
58	            FORCE_RECORD=1
59	            shift
60	            ;;
61	        -h|--help)
62	            sed -n '2,30p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \?//'
63	            exit 0
64	            ;;
65	        *)
66	            echo "error: unknown argument: $1" >&2
67	            exit 2
68	            ;;
69	    esac
70	done
71	
72	# --- Dependencies ------------------------------------------------------------
73	
74	require_cmd() {
75	    if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
76	        echo "error: required command not found: $1" >&2
77	        echo "       install it before running vendor-fetch.sh" >&2
78	        exit 3
79	    fi
80	}
81	
82	require_cmd jq
83	require_cmd git
84	
85	# Pick a sha256 implementation
86	if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
87	    sha256_of() { sha256sum "$1" | awk '{print $1}'; }
88	elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
89	    sha256_of() { shasum -a 256 "$1" | awk '{print $1}'; }
90	else
91	    echo "error: neither sha256sum nor shasum found" >&2
92	    exit 3
93	fi
94	
95	# --- Platform detection (matches version.json platforms.<platform>) ---------
96	
97	detect_platform() {
98	    local os arch
99	    case "$(uname -s)" in
100	        Darwin)  os="darwin" ;;
101	        Linux)   os="linux" ;;
102	        *) echo "unsupported-$(uname -s)" ; return ;;
103	    esac
104	    case "$(uname -m)" in
105	        arm64|aarch64) arch="arm64" ;;
106	        x86_64|amd64)  arch="x86_64" ;;
107	        *) echo "unsupported-$(uname -m)" ; return ;;
108	    esac
109	    echo "${os}-${arch}"
110	}
111	
112	PLATFORM="$(detect_platform)"
113	echo "vendor-fetch: platform=$PLATFORM mode=$MODE"
114	
115	# --- Load active.env so we know which versions to fetch ---------------------
116	
117	if [ ! -f "$ACTIVE_ENV" ]; then
118	    echo "error: $ACTIVE_ENV not found" >&2
119	    exit 4
120	fi
121	
122	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
123	. "$ACTIVE_ENV"
124	
125	# Build the (tool, version) list. The mapping below is the only
126	# place where tool names live; adding a new tool means adding one
127	# line here plus a new SW_<TOOL>_VERSION variable in active.env.
128	TOOLS=(
129	    "sw-cx24:${SW_CX24_VERSION:-}"
130	    "sw-asx24:${SW_ASX24_VERSION:-}"
131	    "sw-em24:${SW_EM24_VERSION:-}"
132	)
133	
134	# --- Helpers -----------------------------------------------------------------
135	
136	manifest_path() {
137	    # $1=tool $2=version
138	    echo "$VENDOR_DIR/$1/$2/version.json"
139	}
140	
141	manifest_get() {
142	    # $1=manifest path $2=jq filter (e.g. .commit)
143	    jq -r "$2" "$1"
144	}
145	
146	resolve_upstream_repo() {
147	    # Echoes a path to a working upstream checkout. Tries the
148	    # repo_path_local first; falls back to a temp clone of `repo`.
149	    # $1=tool $2=manifest_path
150	    local tool manifest local_path repo
151	    tool="$1"
152	    manifest="$2"
153	    local_path="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .repo_path_local)"
154	    repo="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .repo)"
155	
156	    if [ "$local_path" != "null" ] && [ "$local_path" != "TBD" ]; then
157	        local abs="$REPO_ROOT/$local_path"
158	        if [ -d "$abs/.git" ]; then
159	            echo "$abs"
160	            return 0
161	        fi
162	    fi
163	
164	    if [ "$repo" = "null" ] || [ "$repo" = "TBD" ]; then
165	        echo "error: $tool: no working repo_path_local and no canonical 'repo' URL set in $manifest" >&2
166	        return 4
167	    fi
168	
169	    local tmp
170	    tmp="$(mktemp -d -t "vendor-fetch-${tool}.XXXXXX")"
171	    echo "vendor-fetch: $tool: cloning $repo into $tmp" >&2
172	    git clone --quiet "$repo" "$tmp" >&2
173	    echo "$tmp"
174	}
175	
176	build_at_commit() {
177	    # Checkout the recorded commit in the upstream working tree
178	    # and run the build command. Echoes the absolute path to the
179	    # produced binary.
180	    # $1=tool $2=manifest $3=upstream_dir
181	    local tool manifest upstream commit build_cmd binary_src
182	    tool="$1"
183	    manifest="$2"
184	    upstream="$3"
185	    commit="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .commit)"
186	    build_cmd="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .build_cmd)"
187	    binary_src="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .binary_src)"
188	
189	    if [ "$commit" = "null" ] || [ "$commit" = "TBD" ]; then
190	        echo "error: $tool: manifest has no pinned commit" >&2
191	        return 5
192	    fi
193	
194	    echo "vendor-fetch: $tool: checking out $commit in $upstream" >&2
195	    (
196	        cd "$upstream"
197	        # Stash any local mods so we don't clobber the user's
198	        # in-progress work.
199	        git stash push --quiet --include-untracked --message "vendor-fetch.sh autostash" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
200	        git -c advice.detachedHead=false checkout --quiet "$commit"
201	        echo "vendor-fetch: $tool: building with: $build_cmd" >&2
202	        eval "$build_cmd" >&2
203	    )
204	
205	    echo "$upstream/$binary_src"
206	}
207	
208	copy_aux_files() {
209	    # Copy headers_src and docs_src from upstream into the
210	    # versioned vendor dir. These are tracked.
211	    # $1=manifest $2=upstream $3=vendor_versioned_dir
212	    local manifest upstream dest headers_src docs_src
213	    manifest="$1"
214	    upstream="$2"
215	    dest="$3"
216	    headers_src="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .headers_src)"
217	    docs_src="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .docs_src)"
218	
219	    if [ "$headers_src" != "null" ] && [ "$headers_src" != "TBD" ] \
220	       && [ -d "$upstream/$headers_src" ]; then
221	        rm -rf "$dest/includes"
222	        mkdir -p "$dest/includes"
223	        cp -R "$upstream/$headers_src/." "$dest/includes/"
224	    fi
225	
226	    if [ "$docs_src" != "null" ] && [ "$docs_src" != "TBD" ] \
227	       && [ -d "$upstream/$docs_src" ]; then
228	        rm -rf "$dest/docs"
229	        mkdir -p "$dest/docs"
230	        cp -R "$upstream/$docs_src/." "$dest/docs/"
231	    fi
232	}
233	
234	write_recorded_sha() {
235	    # Update version.json with sha256, size, recorded_at,
236	    # recorded_by for the current platform.
237	    # $1=manifest $2=sha $3=size
238	    local manifest sha size now tmp
239	    manifest="$1"
240	    sha="$2"
241	    size="$3"
242	    now="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
243	    tmp="$(mktemp)"
244	    jq \
245	        --arg platform "$PLATFORM" \
246	        --arg sha "$sha" \
247	        --argjson size "$size" \
248	        --arg now "$now" \
249	        '.platforms[$platform] = {sha256: $sha, size: $size}
250	         | .recorded_at = $now
251	         | .recorded_by = "vendor-fetch.sh --record"' \
252	        "$manifest" > "$tmp"
253	    mv "$tmp" "$manifest"
254	}
255	
256	# --- Per-tool fetch ---------------------------------------------------------
257	
258	fetch_tool() {
259	    # $1=tool $2=version
260	    local tool version manifest dest_dir bin_path expected_sha actual_sha size upstream produced
261	    tool="$1"
262	    version="$2"
263	
264	    if [ -z "$version" ]; then
265	        echo "vendor-fetch: $tool: no version pinned in active.env, skipping" >&2
266	        return 0
267	    fi
268	
269	    manifest="$(manifest_path "$tool" "$version")"
270	    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
271	        echo "error: $tool: manifest not found at $manifest" >&2
272	        return 6
273	    fi
274	
275	    dest_dir="$VENDOR_DIR/$tool/$version"
276	    bin_path="$dest_dir/bin/$tool"
277	    expected_sha="$(manifest_get "$manifest" ".platforms[\"$PLATFORM\"].sha256 // \"\"")"
278	
279	    case "$MODE" in
280	    verify)
281	        if [ -z "$expected_sha" ] || [ "$expected_sha" = "TBD" ]; then
282	            echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: no recorded SHA for $PLATFORM"
283	            echo "             run: ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --record $tool"
284	            return 0
285	        fi
286	
287	        # Fast path: binary already present and good.
288	        if [ -f "$bin_path" ]; then
289	            actual_sha="$(sha256_of "$bin_path")"
290	            if [ "$actual_sha" = "$expected_sha" ]; then
291	                echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: ok ($expected_sha)"
292	                return 0
293	            fi
294	            echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: SHA mismatch, rebuilding"
295	            echo "             expected: $expected_sha"
296	            echo "             actual:   $actual_sha"
297	            rm -f "$bin_path"
298	        else
299	            echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: no binary, building"
300	        fi
301	
302	        upstream="$(resolve_upstream_repo "$tool" "$manifest")"
303	        produced="$(build_at_commit "$tool" "$manifest" "$upstream")"
304	        mkdir -p "$dest_dir/bin"
305	        cp "$produced" "$bin_path"
306	        chmod +x "$bin_path"
307	        copy_aux_files "$manifest" "$upstream" "$dest_dir"
308	
309	        actual_sha="$(sha256_of "$bin_path")"
310	        if [ "$actual_sha" != "$expected_sha" ]; then
311	            echo "error: $tool $version: build produced wrong SHA on $PLATFORM" >&2
312	            echo "       expected: $expected_sha" >&2
313	            echo "       actual:   $actual_sha" >&2
314	            echo "       investigate: was the build deterministic? is the manifest stale?" >&2
315	            return 7
316	        fi
317	        echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: ok ($expected_sha)"
318	        ;;
319	
320	    record)
321	        # Only act on the requested tool.
322	        if [ "$tool" != "$RECORD_TOOL" ]; then
323	            return 0
324	        fi
325	
326	        if [ -n "$expected_sha" ] && [ "$expected_sha" != "TBD" ] && [ "$FORCE_RECORD" -ne 1 ]; then
327	            echo "error: $tool $version: SHA already recorded for $PLATFORM ($expected_sha)" >&2
328	            echo "       use --force to overwrite, or bump to a new version directory" >&2
329	            return 8
330	        fi
331	
332	        upstream="$(resolve_upstream_repo "$tool" "$manifest")"
333	        produced="$(build_at_commit "$tool" "$manifest" "$upstream")"
334	        mkdir -p "$dest_dir/bin"
335	        cp "$produced" "$bin_path"
336	        chmod +x "$bin_path"
337	        copy_aux_files "$manifest" "$upstream" "$dest_dir"
338	
339	        actual_sha="$(sha256_of "$bin_path")"
340	        size="$(wc -c < "$bin_path" | tr -d ' ')"
341	        write_recorded_sha "$manifest" "$actual_sha" "$size"
342	        echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: recorded $actual_sha ($size bytes) for $PLATFORM"
343	        echo "             review: git diff $manifest"
344	        ;;
345	    esac
346	}
347	
348	# --- Main loop --------------------------------------------------------------
349	
350	found_record_target=0
351	for entry in "${TOOLS[@]}"; do
352	    tool="${entry%%:*}"
353	    version="${entry#*:}"
354	    if [ "$MODE" = "record" ] && [ "$tool" = "$RECORD_TOOL" ]; then
355	        found_record_target=1
356	    fi
357	    fetch_tool "$tool" "$version"
358	done
359	
360	if [ "$MODE" = "record" ] && [ "$found_record_target" -eq 0 ]; then
361	    echo "error: --record target '$RECORD_TOOL' is not in the TOOLS list" >&2
362	    exit 9
363	fi
364

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# vendor-fetch.sh -- materialize vendored toolchain binaries from
# the manifests committed under vendor/<tool>/<version>/version.json.
#
# Two modes:
#
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh                  -- verify mode (default)
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --record <tool>  -- first-time pin a SHA-256
#
# Verify mode is what runs on every fresh clone or after pulling
# new commits. For each tool listed in vendor/active.env, it makes
# sure the binary at vendor/<tool>/<version>/bin/<tool> exists and
# matches the SHA-256 in version.json. If the binary is missing or
# stale, it rebuilds at the recorded commit and re-verifies.
#
# Record mode is for first-time vendoring of a new tool/version,
# before any SHA-256 has been pinned. It rebuilds at the recorded
# commit, then *writes* the resulting SHA-256 into version.json.
#
# Critical guarantee: builds always happen at the commit recorded
# in version.json, never at upstream HEAD. This is what makes
# rollback and controlled adoption possible.
#
# Dependencies: bash 4+, jq, git, sha256sum (or shasum -a 256).
#
# See docs/vendor-plan.md for the broader design.

set -euo pipefail

# --- Locate ourselves and the repo root --------------------------------------

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
VENDOR_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/vendor"
ACTIVE_ENV="$VENDOR_DIR/active.env"

cd "$REPO_ROOT"

# --- Argument parsing --------------------------------------------------------

MODE="verify"
RECORD_TOOL=""
FORCE_RECORD=0

while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    case "$1" in
        --record)
            MODE="record"
            RECORD_TOOL="${2:-}"
            if [ -z "$RECORD_TOOL" ]; then
                echo "error: --record requires a tool name (e.g. --record sw-cx24)" >&2
                exit 2
            fi
            shift 2
            ;;
        --force)
            FORCE_RECORD=1
            shift
            ;;
        -h|--help)
            sed -n '2,30p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \?//'
            exit 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "error: unknown argument: $1" >&2
            exit 2
            ;;
    esac
done

# --- Dependencies ------------------------------------------------------------

require_cmd() {
    if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "error: required command not found: $1" >&2
        echo "       install it before running vendor-fetch.sh" >&2
        exit 3
    fi
}

require_cmd jq
require_cmd git

# Pick a sha256 implementation
if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    sha256_of() { sha256sum "$1" | awk '{print $1}'; }
elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    sha256_of() { shasum -a 256 "$1" | awk '{print $1}'; }
else
    echo "error: neither sha256sum nor shasum found" >&2
    exit 3
fi

# --- Platform detection (matches version.json platforms.<platform>) ---------

detect_platform() {
    local os arch
    case "$(uname -s)" in
        Darwin)  os="darwin" ;;
        Linux)   os="linux" ;;
        *) echo "unsupported-$(uname -s)" ; return ;;
    esac
    case "$(uname -m)" in
        arm64|aarch64) arch="arm64" ;;
        x86_64|amd64)  arch="x86_64" ;;
        *) echo "unsupported-$(uname -m)" ; return ;;
    esac
    echo "${os}-${arch}"
}

PLATFORM="$(detect_platform)"
echo "vendor-fetch: platform=$PLATFORM mode=$MODE"

# --- Load active.env so we know which versions to fetch ---------------------

if [ ! -f "$ACTIVE_ENV" ]; then
    echo "error: $ACTIVE_ENV not found" >&2
    exit 4
fi

# shellcheck source=/dev/null
. "$ACTIVE_ENV"

# Build the (tool, version) list. The mapping below is the only
# place where tool names live; adding a new tool means adding one
# line here plus a new SW_<TOOL>_VERSION variable in active.env.
TOOLS=(
    "sw-cx24:${SW_CX24_VERSION:-}"
    "sw-asx24:${SW_ASX24_VERSION:-}"
    "sw-em24:${SW_EM24_VERSION:-}"
)

# --- Helpers -----------------------------------------------------------------

manifest_path() {
    # $1=tool $2=version
    echo "$VENDOR_DIR/$1/$2/version.json"
}

manifest_get() {
    # $1=manifest path $2=jq filter (e.g. .commit)
    jq -r "$2" "$1"
}

resolve_upstream_repo() {
    # Echoes a path to a working upstream checkout. Tries the
    # repo_path_local first; falls back to a temp clone of `repo`.
    # $1=tool $2=manifest_path
    local tool manifest local_path repo
    tool="$1"
    manifest="$2"
    local_path="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .repo_path_local)"
    repo="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .repo)"

    if [ "$local_path" != "null" ] && [ "$local_path" != "TBD" ]; then
        local abs="$REPO_ROOT/$local_path"
        if [ -d "$abs/.git" ]; then
            echo "$abs"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    if [ "$repo" = "null" ] || [ "$repo" = "TBD" ]; then
        echo "error: $tool: no working repo_path_local and no canonical 'repo' URL set in $manifest" >&2
        return 4
    fi

    local tmp
    tmp="$(mktemp -d -t "vendor-fetch-${tool}.XXXXXX")"
    echo "vendor-fetch: $tool: cloning $repo into $tmp" >&2
    git clone --quiet "$repo" "$tmp" >&2
    echo "$tmp"
}

build_at_commit() {
    # Checkout the recorded commit in the upstream working tree
    # and run the build command. Echoes the absolute path to the
    # produced binary.
    # $1=tool $2=manifest $3=upstream_dir
    local tool manifest upstream commit build_cmd binary_src
    tool="$1"
    manifest="$2"
    upstream="$3"
    commit="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .commit)"
    build_cmd="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .build_cmd)"
    binary_src="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .binary_src)"

    if [ "$commit" = "null" ] || [ "$commit" = "TBD" ]; then
        echo "error: $tool: manifest has no pinned commit" >&2
        return 5
    fi

    echo "vendor-fetch: $tool: checking out $commit in $upstream" >&2
    (
        cd "$upstream"
        # Stash any local mods so we don't clobber the user's
        # in-progress work.
        git stash push --quiet --include-untracked --message "vendor-fetch.sh autostash" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        git -c advice.detachedHead=false checkout --quiet "$commit"
        echo "vendor-fetch: $tool: building with: $build_cmd" >&2
        eval "$build_cmd" >&2
    )

    echo "$upstream/$binary_src"
}

copy_aux_files() {
    # Copy headers_src and docs_src from upstream into the
    # versioned vendor dir. These are tracked.
    # $1=manifest $2=upstream $3=vendor_versioned_dir
    local manifest upstream dest headers_src docs_src
    manifest="$1"
    upstream="$2"
    dest="$3"
    headers_src="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .headers_src)"
    docs_src="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .docs_src)"

    if [ "$headers_src" != "null" ] && [ "$headers_src" != "TBD" ] \
       && [ -d "$upstream/$headers_src" ]; then
        rm -rf "$dest/includes"
        mkdir -p "$dest/includes"
        cp -R "$upstream/$headers_src/." "$dest/includes/"
    fi

    if [ "$docs_src" != "null" ] && [ "$docs_src" != "TBD" ] \
       && [ -d "$upstream/$docs_src" ]; then
        rm -rf "$dest/docs"
        mkdir -p "$dest/docs"
        cp -R "$upstream/$docs_src/." "$dest/docs/"
    fi
}

write_recorded_sha() {
    # Update version.json with sha256, size, recorded_at,
    # recorded_by for the current platform.
    # $1=manifest $2=sha $3=size
    local manifest sha size now tmp
    manifest="$1"
    sha="$2"
    size="$3"
    now="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    jq \
        --arg platform "$PLATFORM" \
        --arg sha "$sha" \
        --argjson size "$size" \
        --arg now "$now" \
        '.platforms[$platform] = {sha256: $sha, size: $size}
         | .recorded_at = $now
         | .recorded_by = "vendor-fetch.sh --record"' \
        "$manifest" > "$tmp"
    mv "$tmp" "$manifest"
}

# --- Per-tool fetch ---------------------------------------------------------

fetch_tool() {
    # $1=tool $2=version
    local tool version manifest dest_dir bin_path expected_sha actual_sha size upstream produced
    tool="$1"
    version="$2"

    if [ -z "$version" ]; then
        echo "vendor-fetch: $tool: no version pinned in active.env, skipping" >&2
        return 0
    fi

    manifest="$(manifest_path "$tool" "$version")"
    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
        echo "error: $tool: manifest not found at $manifest" >&2
        return 6
    fi

    dest_dir="$VENDOR_DIR/$tool/$version"
    bin_path="$dest_dir/bin/$tool"
    expected_sha="$(manifest_get "$manifest" ".platforms[\"$PLATFORM\"].sha256 // \"\"")"

    case "$MODE" in
    verify)
        if [ -z "$expected_sha" ] || [ "$expected_sha" = "TBD" ]; then
            echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: no recorded SHA for $PLATFORM"
            echo "             run: ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --record $tool"
            return 0
        fi

        # Fast path: binary already present and good.
        if [ -f "$bin_path" ]; then
            actual_sha="$(sha256_of "$bin_path")"
            if [ "$actual_sha" = "$expected_sha" ]; then
                echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: ok ($expected_sha)"
                return 0
            fi
            echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: SHA mismatch, rebuilding"
            echo "             expected: $expected_sha"
            echo "             actual:   $actual_sha"
            rm -f "$bin_path"
        else
            echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: no binary, building"
        fi

        upstream="$(resolve_upstream_repo "$tool" "$manifest")"
        produced="$(build_at_commit "$tool" "$manifest" "$upstream")"
        mkdir -p "$dest_dir/bin"
        cp "$produced" "$bin_path"
        chmod +x "$bin_path"
        copy_aux_files "$manifest" "$upstream" "$dest_dir"

        actual_sha="$(sha256_of "$bin_path")"
        if [ "$actual_sha" != "$expected_sha" ]; then
            echo "error: $tool $version: build produced wrong SHA on $PLATFORM" >&2
            echo "       expected: $expected_sha" >&2
            echo "       actual:   $actual_sha" >&2
            echo "       investigate: was the build deterministic? is the manifest stale?" >&2
            return 7
        fi
        echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: ok ($expected_sha)"
        ;;

    record)
        # Only act on the requested tool.
        if [ "$tool" != "$RECORD_TOOL" ]; then
            return 0
        fi

        if [ -n "$expected_sha" ] && [ "$expected_sha" != "TBD" ] && [ "$FORCE_RECORD" -ne 1 ]; then
            echo "error: $tool $version: SHA already recorded for $PLATFORM ($expected_sha)" >&2
            echo "       use --force to overwrite, or bump to a new version directory" >&2
            return 8
        fi

        upstream="$(resolve_upstream_repo "$tool" "$manifest")"
        produced="$(build_at_commit "$tool" "$manifest" "$upstream")"
        mkdir -p "$dest_dir/bin"
        cp "$produced" "$bin_path"
        chmod +x "$bin_path"
        copy_aux_files "$manifest" "$upstream" "$dest_dir"

        actual_sha="$(sha256_of "$bin_path")"
        size="$(wc -c < "$bin_path" | tr -d ' ')"
        write_recorded_sha "$manifest" "$actual_sha" "$size"
        echo "vendor-fetch: $tool $version: recorded $actual_sha ($size bytes) for $PLATFORM"
        echo "             review: git diff $manifest"
        ;;
    esac
}

# --- Main loop --------------------------------------------------------------

found_record_target=0
for entry in "${TOOLS[@]}"; do
    tool="${entry%%:*}"
    version="${entry#*:}"
    if [ "$MODE" = "record" ] && [ "$tool" = "$RECORD_TOOL" ]; then
        found_record_target=1
    fi
    fetch_tool "$tool" "$version"
done

if [ "$MODE" = "record" ] && [ "$found_record_target" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "error: --record target '$RECORD_TOOL' is not in the TOOLS list" >&2
    exit 9
fi

content.text: Now let me create the vendor-fetch script adapted for this project's tools, and the build/run scripts.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# vendor-fetch.sh -- materialize vendored toolchain artifacts from
# the manifests committed under vendor/<tool>/<version>/version.json.
#
# For the OCaml project, tools are a mix of:
#   - Host binaries (pa24r, pl24r, cor24-run) built with cargo
#   - COR24 artifacts (p24p.s, pvm.s, runtime.spc) copied as-is
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh           -- fetch all tools
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --check   -- verify artifacts exist
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --help
#
# Dependencies: bash, jq, git, cargo (for host binaries).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
VENDOR_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/vendor"
ACTIVE_ENV="$VENDOR_DIR/active.env"

cd "$REPO_ROOT"

# --- Argument parsing -------------------------------------------------------

MODE="fetch"

while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    case "$1" in
        --check)
            MODE="check"
            shift
            ;;
        -h|--help)
            sed -n '2,15p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \?//'
            exit 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "error: unknown argument: $1" >&2
            exit 2
            ;;
    esac
done

# --- Load active.env -------------------------------------------------------

if [ ! -f "$ACTIVE_ENV" ]; then
    echo "error: $ACTIVE_ENV not found" >&2
    exit 4
fi

# shellcheck source=/dev/null
. "$ACTIVE_ENV"

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

manifest_get() {
    jq -r "$2" "$1"
}

resolve_local_repo() {
    # $1=manifest path
    local local_path abs
    local_path="$(manifest_get "$1" .repo_path_local)"
    if [ "$local_path" != "null" ] && [ "$local_path" != "TBD" ]; then
        abs="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-pascal/v0.1.0/$local_path"
        # Try relative to vendor dir first, then relative to repo root
        if [ -d "$abs/.git" ]; then
            echo "$abs"
            return 0
        fi
        abs="$REPO_ROOT/$local_path"
        if [ -d "$abs/.git" ]; then
            echo "$abs"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    echo ""
}

# --- Fetch Pascal compiler artifacts ---------------------------------------

fetch_pascal() {
    local version="$SW_PASCAL_VERSION"
    local dest="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-pascal/$version"
    local manifest="$dest/version.json"
    local upstream

    echo "--- sw-pascal $version ---"

    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
        echo "error: manifest not found: $manifest" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    upstream="$(resolve_local_repo "$manifest")"
    if [ -z "$upstream" ]; then
        echo "error: sw-pascal: cannot resolve upstream repo" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local commit
    commit="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .commit)"

    # Pascal compiler is p24p.s (COR24 assembly, not a host binary)
    local p24p_src="$upstream/compiler/p24p.s"
    local runtime_spc_src="$upstream/runtime/runtime.spc"
    local runtime_spi_src="$upstream/runtime/runtime.spi"

    if [ "$MODE" = "check" ]; then
        local ok=0
        [ -f "$dest/bin/p24p.s" ] && echo "  p24p.s: ok" || { echo "  p24p.s: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        [ -f "$dest/bin/runtime.spc" ] && echo "  runtime.spc: ok" || { echo "  runtime.spc: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        return $ok
    fi

    echo "  upstream: $upstream (commit $commit)"

    if [ -f "$p24p_src" ]; then
        cp "$p24p_src" "$dest/bin/p24p.s"
        echo "  copied: p24p.s"
    else
        echo "  warning: p24p.s not found at $p24p_src" >&2
    fi

    if [ -f "$runtime_spc_src" ]; then
        cp "$runtime_spc_src" "$dest/bin/runtime.spc"
        echo "  copied: runtime.spc"
    else
        echo "  warning: runtime.spc not found at $runtime_spc_src" >&2
    fi

    if [ -f "$runtime_spi_src" ]; then
        cp "$runtime_spi_src" "$dest/bin/runtime.spi"
        echo "  copied: runtime.spi"
    else
        echo "  info: runtime.spi not found (optional)"
    fi
}

# --- Fetch P-code toolchain artifacts ---------------------------------------

fetch_pcode() {
    local version="$SW_PCODE_VERSION"
    local dest="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-pcode/$version"
    local manifest="$dest/version.json"
    local upstream

    echo "--- sw-pcode $version ---"

    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
        echo "error: manifest not found: $manifest" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    upstream="$(resolve_local_repo "$manifest")"
    if [ -z "$upstream" ]; then
        echo "error: sw-pcode: cannot resolve upstream repo" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local commit
    commit="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .commit)"

    if [ "$MODE" = "check" ]; then
        local ok=0
        [ -f "$dest/bin/pvm.s" ] && echo "  pvm.s: ok" || { echo "  pvm.s: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        [ -f "$dest/bin/pa24r" ] && echo "  pa24r: ok" || { echo "  pa24r: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        [ -f "$dest/bin/pl24r" ] && echo "  pl24r: ok" || { echo "  pl24r: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        return $ok
    fi

    echo "  upstream: $upstream (commit $commit)"

    # pvm.s is a COR24 assembly file, copy directly
    local pvm_src="$upstream/vm/pvm.s"
    if [ -f "$pvm_src" ]; then
        cp "$pvm_src" "$dest/bin/pvm.s"
        echo "  copied: pvm.s"
    else
        echo "  warning: pvm.s not found at $pvm_src" >&2
    fi

    # pa24r and pl24r are host Rust binaries
    local pa24r_src="$upstream/target/release/pa24r"
    local pl24r_src="$upstream/target/release/pl24r"

    if [ -f "$pa24r_src" ]; then
        cp "$pa24r_src" "$dest/bin/pa24r"
        chmod +x "$dest/bin/pa24r"
        echo "  copied: pa24r"
    else
        echo "  warning: pa24r not found at $pa24r_src (run 'cargo build --release' in sw-cor24-pcode)" >&2
    fi

    if [ -f "$pl24r_src" ]; then
        cp "$pl24r_src" "$dest/bin/pl24r"
        chmod +x "$dest/bin/pl24r"
        echo "  copied: pl24r"
    else
        echo "  warning: pl24r not found at $pl24r_src (run 'cargo build --release' in sw-cor24-pcode)" >&2
    fi
}

# --- Fetch emulator ---------------------------------------------------------

fetch_em24() {
    local version="$SW_EM24_VERSION"
    local dest="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-em24/$version"
    local manifest="$dest/version.json"

    echo "--- sw-em24 $version ---"

    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
        echo "error: manifest not found: $manifest" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    if [ "$MODE" = "check" ]; then
        # cor24-run can be system-installed or vendored
        if [ -f "$dest/bin/cor24-run" ] || command -v cor24-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "  cor24-run: ok"
            return 0
        else
            echo "  cor24-run: MISSING"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Try system-installed cor24-run first
    if command -v cor24-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "  cor24-run: using system-installed $(which cor24-run)"
        return 0
    fi

    local upstream
    upstream="$(resolve_local_repo "$manifest")"
    if [ -z "$upstream" ]; then
        echo "error: sw-em24: cannot resolve upstream repo" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local cor24_src="$upstream/target/release/cor24-run"
    if [ -f "$cor24_src" ]; then
        cp "$cor24_src" "$dest/bin/cor24-run"
        chmod +x "$dest/bin/cor24-run"
        echo "  copied: cor24-run"
    else
        echo "  warning: cor24-run not found at $cor24_src (run 'cargo build --release' in sw-cor24-emulator)" >&2
    fi
}

# --- Main -------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "vendor-fetch: mode=$MODE"
echo ""

fetch_pascal
echo ""
fetch_pcode
echo ""
fetch_em24
echo ""

echo "vendor-fetch: done"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# vendor-fetch.sh -- materialize vendored toolchain artifacts from
# the manifests committed under vendor/<tool>/<version>/version.json.
#
# For the OCaml project, tools are a mix of:
#   - Host binaries (pa24r, pl24r, cor24-run) built with cargo
#   - COR24 artifacts (p24p.s, pvm.s, runtime.spc) copied as-is
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh           -- fetch all tools
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --check   -- verify artifacts exist
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --help
#
# Dependencies: bash, jq, git, cargo (for host binaries).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
VENDOR_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/vendor"
ACTIVE_ENV="$VENDOR_DIR/active.env"

cd "$REPO_ROOT"

# --- Argument parsing -------------------------------------------------------

MODE="fetch"

while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    case "$1" in
        --check)
            MODE="check"
            shift
            ;;
        -h|--help)
            sed -n '2,15p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \?//'
            exit 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "error: unknown argument: $1" >&2
            exit 2
            ;;
    esac
done

# --- Load active.env -------------------------------------------------------

if [ ! -f "$ACTIVE_ENV" ]; then
    echo "error: $ACTIVE_ENV not found" >&2
    exit 4
fi

# shellcheck source=/dev/null
. "$ACTIVE_ENV"

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

manifest_get() {
    jq -r "$2" "$1"
}

resolve_local_repo() {
    # $1=manifest path
    local local_path abs
    local_path="$(manifest_get "$1" .repo_path_local)"
    if [ "$local_path" != "null" ] && [ "$local_path" != "TBD" ]; then
        abs="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-pascal/v0.1.0/$local_path"
        # Try relative to vendor dir first, then relative to repo root
        if [ -d "$abs/.git" ]; then
            echo "$abs"
            return 0
        fi
        abs="$REPO_ROOT/$local_path"
        if [ -d "$abs/.git" ]; then
            echo "$abs"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    echo ""
}

# --- Fetch Pascal compiler artifacts ---------------------------------------

fetch_pascal() {
    local version="$SW_PASCAL_VERSION"
    local dest="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-pascal/$version"
    local manifest="$dest/version.json"
    local upstream

    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
        echo "error: manifest not found: $manifest" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    upstream="$(resolve_local_repo "$manifest")"
    if [ -z "$upstream" ]; then
        echo "error: sw-pascal: cannot resolve upstream repo" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local commit

    local p24p_src="$upstream/compiler/p24p.s"
    local runtime_spc_src="$upstream/runtime/runtime.spc"
    local runtime_spi_src="$upstream/runtime/runtime.spi"

    if [ "$MODE" = "check" ]; then
        local ok=0
        [ -f "$dest/bin/p24p.s" ] && echo "  p24p.s: ok" || { echo "  p24p.s: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        [ -f "$dest/bin/runtime.spc" ] && echo "  runtime.spc: ok" || { echo "  runtime.spc: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        return $ok
    fi

    echo "  upstream: $upstream (commit $commit)"

    if [ -f "$p24p_src" ]; then
        cp "$p24p_src" "$dest/bin/p24p.s"
        echo "  copied: p24p.s"
    else
        echo "  warning: p24p.s not found at $p24p_src" >&2
    fi

    if [ -f "$runtime_spc_src" ]; then
        cp "$runtime_spc_src" "$dest/bin/runtime.spc"
        echo "  copied: runtime.spc"
    else
        echo "  warning: runtime.spc not found at $runtime_spc_src" >&2
    fi

    if [ -f "$runtime_spi_src" ]; then
        cp "$runtime_spi_src" "$dest/bin/runtime.spi"
        echo "  copied: runtime.spi"
    else
        echo "  info: runtime.spi not found (optional)"
    fi
}

# --- Fetch P-code toolchain artifacts ---------------------------------------

fetch_pcode() {

    fi

    fi

    # pa24r and pl24r are host Rust binaries
    local pa24r_src="$upstream/target/release/pa24r"

fetch_em24() {

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys, struct
data = open(sys.argv[1], 'rb').read()
load_addr = int(sys.argv[2], 0) if len(sys.argv) > 2 else 0x010000
magic, ver = data[:4], data[4]
entry = int.from_bytes(data[5:8], 'little')
code_size = int.from_bytes(data[8:11], 'little')
data_size = int.from_bytes(data[11:14], 'little')
global_count = int.from_bytes(data[14:17], 'little')
body = bytearray(data[18:18+code_size+data_size])
total = code_size + data_size
i = 0
while i < code_size:
    op = body[i]
    if op == 0x01 and i + 4 <= code_size:
        val = int.from_bytes(body[i+1:i+4], 'little')
        if code_size <= val < total:
            val += load_addr
            body[i+1:i+4] = val.to_bytes(3, 'little')
        i += 4
    elif op in (0x30,0x31,0x32,0x33,0x54,0x55,0x56): i += 4
    elif op in (0x02,0x34,0x35,0x36,0x40,0x42,0x43,0x44,0x45,0x57,0x60): i += 2
    elif op in (0x58,0x59): i += 3
    elif op == 0x5A: i += 5
    else: i += 1
open(sys.argv[1].replace('.p24', '.bin'), 'wb').write(body)
print(f"Wrote {len(body)} bytes (code={code_size}, data={data_size}, globals={global_count}, entry=0x{entry:06X})")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Relocate a .p24 binary for loading at a given address.

Copied from sw-cor24-pascal/scripts/relocate_p24.py.
Rewrites absolute data-section references in p-code instructions
to account for a non-zero load address.
"""
import sys, struct
data = open(sys.argv[1], 'rb').read()
load_addr = int(sys.argv[2], 0) if len(sys.argv) > 2 else 0x010000
magic, ver = data[:4], data[4]
entry = int.from_bytes(data[5:8], 'little')
code_size = int.from_bytes(data[8:11], 'little')
data_size = int.from_bytes(data[11:14], 'little')
global_count = int.from_bytes(data[14:17], 'little')
body = bytearray(data[18:18+code_size+data_size])
total = code_size + data_size
i = 0
while i < code_size:
    op = body[i]
    if op == 0x01 and i + 4 <= code_size:
        val = int.from_bytes(body[i+1:i+4], 'little')
        if code_size <= val < total:
            val += load_addr
            body[i+1:i+4] = val.to_bytes(3, 'little')
        i += 4
    elif op in (0x30,0x31,0x32,0x33,0x54,0x55,0x56): i += 4
    elif op in (0x02,0x34,0x35,0x36,0x40,0x42,0x43,0x44,0x45,0x57,0x60): i += 2
    elif op in (0x58,0x59): i += 3
    elif op == 0x5A: i += 5
    else: i += 1
open(sys.argv[1].replace('.p24', '.bin'), 'wb').write(body)
print(f"Wrote {len(body)} bytes (code={code_size}, data={data_size}, globals={global_count}, entry=0x{entry:06X})")

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# run-pascal.sh -- Compile and run a Pascal program through the full
# p24p toolchain using vendored tools.
#
# Pipeline: .pas -> p24p -> .spc -> pl24r (link) -> pa24r -> .p24 -> pvm.s
#
# Usage: ./scripts/run-pascal.sh <file.pas> [max_instructions]
set -euo pipefail

PAS="${1:?Usage: $0 <file.pas> [max_instructions]}"
MAX_INSTRS="${2:-50000000}"

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

# --- Load vendored tool versions ---
. "$REPO_ROOT/vendor/active.env"

# --- Tool paths (vendored) ---
P24P_S="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pascal/$SW_PASCAL_VERSION/bin/p24p.s"
RUNTIME="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pascal/$SW_PASCAL_VERSION/bin/runtime.spc"
PVM="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pvm.s"
PL24R="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pl24r"
PA24R="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pa24r"

# cor24-run: prefer vendored, fall back to system
COR24_RUN="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-em24/$SW_EM24_VERSION/bin/cor24-run"
if [ ! -f "$COR24_RUN" ]; then
    COR24_RUN="$(command -v cor24-run 2>/dev/null || true)"
fi
if [ -z "$COR24_RUN" ]; then
    echo "error: cor24-run not found (vendored or system)" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# --- Verify tools exist ---
for tool in "$P24P_S" "$RUNTIME" "$PVM" "$PL24R" "$PA24R"; do
    if [ ! -f "$tool" ]; then
        echo "error: missing tool: $tool" >&2
        echo "       run: ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

NAME=$(basename "$PAS" .pas)
TMP="/tmp/p24p_ocaml_$$"
mkdir -p "$TMP"
trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT

# Resolve code_ptr address dynamically from PVM
CODE_PTR_ADDR=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$PVM" -e code_ptr --speed 0 -n 0 2>&1 | \
  grep "Entry point:" | sed 's/.*@ //')
if [ -z "$CODE_PTR_ADDR" ]; then
  echo "Error: could not resolve code_ptr address from PVM" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Step 1: Compile Pascal to .spc
SPC_OUTPUT=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$P24P_S" -u "$(cat "$PAS")"$'\x04' --speed 0 -n 50000000 2>&1 | \
  grep -v '^\[UART' | sed 's/^UART output: //')

if ! echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | grep -q "; OK"; then
  echo "Compilation failed:" >&2
  echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | grep "error" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | sed -n '/^\.module/,/^\.endmodule/p' > "$TMP/$NAME.spc"

# Step 2: Link with runtime
"$PL24R" "$RUNTIME" "$TMP/$NAME.spc" -o "$TMP/${NAME}_linked.spc" 2>/dev/null

# Step 3: Assemble to .p24
"$PA24R" "$TMP/${NAME}_linked.spc" -o "$TMP/$NAME.p24" 2>/dev/null

# Step 4: Relocate for load address 0x010000
python3 "$SCRIPT_DIR/relocate_p24.py" "$TMP/$NAME.p24" 0x010000 >/dev/null

# Step 5: Create code_ptr patch (0x010000 LE)
printf '\x00\x00\x01' > "$TMP/code_ptr.bin"

# Step 6: Run on PVM
"$COR24_RUN" --run "$PVM" \
  --load-binary "$TMP/$NAME.bin@0x010000" \
  --load-binary "$TMP/code_ptr.bin@${CODE_PTR_ADDR}" \
  --terminal --speed 0 -n "$MAX_INSTRS" 2>&1 | \
  grep -v '^\[' | grep -v '^Assembled' | grep -v '^Running' | \
  grep -v '^Executed' | grep -v '^Loaded' | grep -v '^PVM OK' | \
  grep -v '^$' | grep -v '^HALT$'

# p24p toolchain using vendored tools.
#
# Pipeline: .pas -> p24p -> .spc -> pl24r (link) -> pa24r -> .p24 -> pvm.s
#
# Usage: ./scripts/run-pascal.sh <file.pas> [max_instructions]
set -euo pipefail

PAS="${1:?Usage: $0 <file.pas> [max_instructions]}"
MAX_INSTRS="${2:-50000000}"

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

# --- Load vendored tool versions ---
. "$REPO_ROOT/vendor/active.env"

# --- Tool paths (vendored) ---
P24P_S="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pascal/$SW_PASCAL_VERSION/bin/p24p.s"
RUNTIME="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pascal/$SW_PASCAL_VERSION/bin/runtime.spc"
PVM="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pvm.s"
PL24R="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pl24r"
PA24R="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pa24r"

# cor24-run: prefer vendored, fall back to system
COR24_RUN="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-em24/$SW_EM24_VERSION/bin/cor24-run"
if [ ! -f "$COR24_RUN" ]; then
    COR24_RUN="$(command -v cor24-run 2>/dev/null || true)"
fi
if [ -z "$COR24_RUN" ]; then
    echo "error: cor24-run not found (vendored or system)" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# --- Verify tools exist ---
for tool in "$P24P_S" "$RUNTIME" "$PVM" "$PL24R" "$PA24R"; do
    if [ ! -f "$tool" ]; then
        echo "error: missing tool: $tool" >&2
        echo "       run: ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

NAME=$(basename "$PAS" .pas)
TMP="/tmp/p24p_ocaml_$$"
mkdir -p "$TMP"
trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT

# Resolve code_ptr address dynamically from PVM
CODE_PTR_ADDR=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$PVM" -e code_ptr --speed 0 -n 0 2>&1 | \
  grep "Entry point:" | sed 's/.*@ //')
if [ -z "$CODE_PTR_ADDR" ]; then
  echo "Error: could not resolve code_ptr address from PVM" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Step 1: Compile Pascal to .spc
SPC_OUTPUT=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$P24P_S" -u "$(cat "$PAS")"$'\x04' --speed 0 -n 50000000 2>&1 | \
  grep -v '^\[UART' | sed 's/^UART output: //')

if ! echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | grep -q "; OK"; then
  echo "Compilation failed:" >&2
  echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | grep "error" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | sed -n '/^\.module/,/^\.endmodule/p' > "$TMP/$NAME.spc"

# Step 2: Link with runtime
"$PL24R" "$RUNTIME" "$TMP/$NAME.spc" -o "$TMP/${NAME}_linked.spc" 2>/dev/null

# Step 3: Assemble to .p24
"$PA24R" "$TMP/${NAME}_linked.spc" -o "$TMP/$NAME.p24" 2>/dev/null

# Step 4: Relocate for load address 0x010000
python3 "$SCRIPT_DIR/relocate_p24.py" "$TMP/$NAME.p24" 0x010000 >/dev/null

# Step 5: Create code_ptr patch (0x010000 LE)
printf '\x00\x00\x01' > "$TMP/code_ptr.bin"

# Step 6: Run on PVM
"$COR24_RUN" --run "$PVM" \
  --load-binary "$TMP/$NAME.bin@0x010000" \
  --load-binary "$TMP/code_ptr.bin@${CODE_PTR_ADDR}" \
  --terminal --speed 0 -n "$MAX_INSTRS" 2>&1 | \
  grep -v '^\[' | grep -v '^Assembled' | grep -v '^Running' | \
  grep -v '^Executed' | grep -v '^Loaded' | grep -v '^PVM OK' | \
  grep -v '^$' | grep -v '^HALT$'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# vendor-fetch.sh -- materialize vendored toolchain artifacts from
# the manifests committed under vendor/<tool>/<version>/version.json.
#
# For the OCaml project, tools are a mix of:
#   - Host binaries (pa24r, pl24r, cor24-run) built with cargo
#   - COR24 artifacts (p24p.s, pvm.s, runtime.spc) copied as-is
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh           -- fetch all tools
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --check   -- verify artifacts exist
#   ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --help
#
# Dependencies: bash, jq, git, cargo (for host binaries).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
VENDOR_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/vendor"
ACTIVE_ENV="$VENDOR_DIR/active.env"

cd "$REPO_ROOT"

# --- Argument parsing -------------------------------------------------------

MODE="fetch"

while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    case "$1" in
        --check)
            MODE="check"
            shift
            ;;
        -h|--help)
            sed -n '2,15p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \?//'
            exit 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "error: unknown argument: $1" >&2
            exit 2
            ;;
    esac
done

# --- Load active.env -------------------------------------------------------

if [ ! -f "$ACTIVE_ENV" ]; then
    echo "error: $ACTIVE_ENV not found" >&2
    exit 4
fi

# shellcheck source=/dev/null
. "$ACTIVE_ENV"

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

manifest_get() {
    jq -r "$2" "$1"
}

resolve_local_repo() {
    # $1=manifest path
    local local_path abs
    local_path="$(manifest_get "$1" .repo_path_local)"
    if [ "$local_path" != "null" ] && [ "$local_path" != "TBD" ]; then
        abs="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-pascal/v0.1.0/$local_path"
        # Try relative to vendor dir first, then relative to repo root
        if [ -d "$abs/.git" ]; then
            echo "$abs"
            return 0
        fi
        abs="$REPO_ROOT/$local_path"
        if [ -d "$abs/.git" ]; then
            echo "$abs"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    echo ""
}

# --- Fetch Pascal compiler artifacts ---------------------------------------

fetch_pascal() {
    local version="$SW_PASCAL_VERSION"
    local dest="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-pascal/$version"
    local manifest="$dest/version.json"
    local upstream

    echo "--- sw-pascal $version ---"

    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
        echo "error: manifest not found: $manifest" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    upstream="$(resolve_local_repo "$manifest")"
    if [ -z "$upstream" ]; then
        echo "error: sw-pascal: cannot resolve upstream repo" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local commit
    commit="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .commit)"

    # Pascal compiler is p24p.s (COR24 assembly, not a host binary)
    local p24p_src="$upstream/compiler/p24p.s"
    local runtime_spc_src="$upstream/runtime/runtime.spc"
    local runtime_spi_src="$upstream/runtime/runtime.spi"

    if [ "$MODE" = "check" ]; then
        local ok=0
        [ -f "$dest/bin/p24p.s" ] && echo "  p24p.s: ok" || { echo "  p24p.s: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        [ -f "$dest/bin/runtime.spc" ] && echo "  runtime.spc: ok" || { echo "  runtime.spc: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        return $ok
    fi

    echo "  upstream: $upstream (commit $commit)"

    if [ -f "$p24p_src" ]; then
        cp "$p24p_src" "$dest/bin/p24p.s"
        echo "  copied: p24p.s"
    else
        echo "  warning: p24p.s not found at $p24p_src" >&2
    fi

    if [ -f "$runtime_spc_src" ]; then
        cp "$runtime_spc_src" "$dest/bin/runtime.spc"
        echo "  copied: runtime.spc"
    else
        echo "  warning: runtime.spc not found at $runtime_spc_src" >&2
    fi

    if [ -f "$runtime_spi_src" ]; then
        cp "$runtime_spi_src" "$dest/bin/runtime.spi"
        echo "  copied: runtime.spi"
    else
        echo "  info: runtime.spi not found (optional)"
    fi
}

# --- Fetch P-code toolchain artifacts ---------------------------------------

fetch_pcode() {
    local version="$SW_PCODE_VERSION"
    local dest="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-pcode/$version"
    local manifest="$dest/version.json"
    local upstream

    echo "--- sw-pcode $version ---"

    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
        echo "error: manifest not found: $manifest" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    upstream="$(resolve_local_repo "$manifest")"
    if [ -z "$upstream" ]; then
        echo "error: sw-pcode: cannot resolve upstream repo" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local commit
    commit="$(manifest_get "$manifest" .commit)"

    if [ "$MODE" = "check" ]; then
        local ok=0
        [ -f "$dest/bin/pvm.s" ] && echo "  pvm.s: ok" || { echo "  pvm.s: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        [ -f "$dest/bin/pa24r" ] && echo "  pa24r: ok" || { echo "  pa24r: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        [ -f "$dest/bin/pl24r" ] && echo "  pl24r: ok" || { echo "  pl24r: MISSING"; ok=1; }
        return $ok
    fi

    echo "  upstream: $upstream (commit $commit)"

    # pvm.s is a COR24 assembly file, copy directly
    local pvm_src="$upstream/vm/pvm.s"
    if [ -f "$pvm_src" ]; then
        cp "$pvm_src" "$dest/bin/pvm.s"
        echo "  copied: pvm.s"
    else
        echo "  warning: pvm.s not found at $pvm_src" >&2
    fi

    # pa24r and pl24r are host Rust binaries
    local pa24r_src="$upstream/target/release/pa24r"
    local pl24r_src="$upstream/target/release/pl24r"

    if [ -f "$pa24r_src" ]; then
        cp "$pa24r_src" "$dest/bin/pa24r"
        chmod +x "$dest/bin/pa24r"
        echo "  copied: pa24r"
    else
        echo "  warning: pa24r not found at $pa24r_src (run 'cargo build --release' in sw-cor24-pcode)" >&2
    fi

    if [ -f "$pl24r_src" ]; then
        cp "$pl24r_src" "$dest/bin/pl24r"
        chmod +x "$dest/bin/pl24r"
        echo "  copied: pl24r"
    else
        echo "  warning: pl24r not found at $pl24r_src (run 'cargo build --release' in sw-cor24-pcode)" >&2
    fi
}

# --- Fetch emulator ---------------------------------------------------------

fetch_em24() {
    local version="$SW_EM24_VERSION"
    local dest="$VENDOR_DIR/sw-em24/$version"
    local manifest="$dest/version.json"

    echo "--- sw-em24 $version ---"

    if [ ! -f "$manifest" ]; then
        echo "error: manifest not found: $manifest" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    if [ "$MODE" = "check" ]; then
        # cor24-run can be system-installed or vendored
        if [ -f "$dest/bin/cor24-run" ] || command -v cor24-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "  cor24-run: ok"
            return 0
        else
            echo "  cor24-run: MISSING"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Try system-installed cor24-run first
    if command -v cor24-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "  cor24-run: using system-installed $(which cor24-run)"
        return 0
    fi

    local upstream
    upstream="$(resolve_local_repo "$manifest")"
    if [ -z "$upstream" ]; then
        echo "error: sw-em24: cannot resolve upstream repo" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local cor24_src="$upstream/target/release/cor24-run"
    if [ -f "$cor24_src" ]; then
        cp "$cor24_src" "$dest/bin/cor24-run"
        chmod +x "$dest/bin/cor24-run"
        echo "  copied: cor24-run"
    else
        echo "  warning: cor24-run not found at $cor24_src (run 'cargo build --release' in sw-cor24-emulator)" >&2
    fi
}

# --- Main -------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "vendor-fetch: mode=$MODE"
echo ""

fetch_pascal
echo ""
fetch_pcode
echo ""
fetch_em24
echo ""

echo "vendor-fetch: done"

# sw-cor24-ocaml -- OCaml interpreter for COR24
# Build system using vendored Pascal toolchain

# Fetch vendored tools from upstream repos
vendor-fetch:
    ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh

# Check that vendored tools are present
vendor-check:
    ./scripts/vendor-fetch.sh --check

# Run a Pascal program through the full toolchain
# Usage: just run-pascal tests/hello.pas
run-pascal file:
    ./scripts/run-pascal.sh {{file}}

# Run regression tests
test:
    REG_RS_DATA_DIR=work/reg-rs reg-rs run -v

# Run regression tests (quiet, for CI)
test-quiet:
    REG_RS_DATA_DIR=work/reg-rs reg-rs run -q

# Show regression test report
test-report:
    REG_RS_DATA_DIR=work/reg-rs reg-rs report -vv

# Smoke test: verify toolchain works
smoke:
    @echo "--- Hello World ---"
    @./scripts/run-pascal.sh tests/hello.pas
    @echo "--- Hello Int ---"
    @./scripts/run-pascal.sh tests/hello_int.pas
    @echo "--- Smoke test passed ---"

- `scripts/vendor-fetch.sh` copies artifacts from local sibling repos

**Build pipeline verified:**

- `just smoke` -- "Hello, World!" and "42" both pass

**Next step** is `002-lexer` (implement the OCaml tokenizer in Pascal). Run `agentrail next` in a new session to pick it up.

# /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.
```

I need to understand what Pascal language features the p24p compiler supports so I can write a lexer in Pascal. Explore /Users/mike/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal thoroughly:

1. Read compiler/docs/ -- especially any language reference, supported features, or limitations

3. Read runtime/runtime.spc or runtime docs to understand what runtime library functions are available (write, writeln, read, new, dispose, etc.)
4. Look at the compiler source (compiler/src/*.c or *.h) for any SUPPORTED_FEATURES or TODO comments that reveal what's implemented

Key questions:
- Does it support variant records (tagged unions)?
- Does it support dynamic allocation (new/dispose)?
- Does it support string types and string operations?
- Does it support enumerated types?
- Does it support case/of statements?
- What's the maximum string length?
- Are there any known limitations?

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me provide a comprehensive report to the user:

## p24p Pascal Compiler — Supported Language Features

Based on my thorough exploration of the p24p compiler source, tests, and documentation, here's what Pascal features are **SUPPORTED** for your lexer:

### **SUPPORTED LANGUAGE FEATURES**

#### **Data Types**
- ✅ `integer` — 24-bit signed integers
- ✅ `boolean` — true/false (represented as 1/0)
- ✅ `char` — single character (8-bit ordinal, 0-255)
- ✅ `array[low..high] of type` — fixed-size arrays with explicit bounds
  - Element types: `integer`, `boolean`, `char`
  - Bounds are literal integers (can be negative)
  - Example: `array[0..9] of integer`, `array[-5..5] of char`
- ✅ `record` — structured types with named fields
  - Syntax: `type Point = record x, y: integer end;`
  - Fields accessed via dot notation: `p.x`
  - Nested field access supported

#### **String Handling**
- ✅ String literals: `'Hello, World!'` (max 256 bytes)
- ✅ Character literals: `'A'` (single quotes, distinguished from strings by length)
- **Maximum string length: 256 bytes** (defined in lexer.h as MAX_STRING)
- Strings stored in data segment, null-terminated
- No string type as a first-class variable type (only literals in expressions)

#### **Type System**
- ✅ Type definitions: `type MyType = <type-denoter>;`
- ✅ Type aliases for arrays, records, and scalar types
- ✅ `char` and `integer` are interchangeable in some contexts (comparison, arithmetic)

#### **Variables & Constants**
- ✅ Variable declarations with explicit types
- ✅ Constant declarations (compile-time evaluation)
- ✅ Global variables and local variables (in procedures/functions)
- ✅ Nested scopes with static link chain for nonlocal access

#### **Control Structures**
- ✅ `if`/`then`/`else` — conditional statements
- ✅ `while`/`do` — pre-test loops
- ✅ `repeat`/`until` — post-test loops
- ✅ `for i := start to end do` — counting loops (also `downto`)
- ✅ `case` expression `of` ... `end` — multi-way branching
  - Case branches: `1: statement;` or `1, 2, 3: statement;`
  - No default clause (unmatchedFallthrough drops selector)
- ✅ `begin`/`end` — compound statements

#### **Procedures & Functions**
- ✅ `procedure` — parameterized subprograms (no return value)
- ✅ `function` — subprograms with return type
- ✅ Parameters by value: `procedure Foo(a, b: integer);`
- ✅ Parameters by reference: `procedure Swap(var x, y: integer);`
- ✅ Nested procedures and functions with full lexical scoping
- ✅ Forward declarations: `function double(n: integer): integer; forward;`
- ✅ Mutual recursion via forward declarations
- ✅ Local variable declarations within procedures/functions

#### **Operators**
- ✅ Arithmetic: `+`, `-`, `*`, `div`, `mod`
- ✅ Unary: `-` (negation), `+` (identity)
- ✅ Comparison: `=`, `<>`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`
- ✅ Logical: `and`, `or`, `not`
- ✅ Assignment: `:=`

#### **I/O Statements**
- ✅ `write(expr1, expr2, ...)` — output without newline
- ✅ `writeln(expr1, ...)` — output with newline
- ✅ `writeln` with formatting: `writeln(n:10)` — right-justified in field width
- ✅ `read(var1, var2, ...)` — input values
- ✅ `readln(var1, ...)` — input then skip to next line
- ✅ `write()` or `writeln()` with no arguments (just newline)

#### **Standard Library Functions**
- ✅ `abs(x)` — absolute value
- ✅ `odd(x)` — test if odd
- ✅ `ord(c)` — character to integer
- ✅ `chr(n)` — integer to character (ordinal value)
- ✅ `succ(x)` — successor (x+1)
- ✅ `pred(x)` — predecessor (x-1)
- ✅ `sqr(x)` — square (x*x)
- ✅ `eof()` — end-of-file test
- ✅ `eoln()` — end-of-line test

#### **Hardware Support**
- ✅ `uses Hardware;` — optional hardware unit
- ✅ `LedOn` / `LedOff` — LED control
- ✅ `ReadSwitch` — read switch state (stub)
- ✅ `Halt` — stop execution

#### **Memory Access (Low-Level)**
- ✅ `peek(addr)` — read word from memory address
- ✅ `poke(addr, val)` — write word to memory address
- ✅ `memcpy(dst, src, count)` — bulk copy
- ✅ `memset(addr, val, count)` — bulk fill

#### **Units/Modules**
- ✅ `uses Hardware;` — include hardware unit
- ✅ `uses units;` — user-defined unit support

#### **Comments**
- ✅ `{ ... }` — brace-delimited comments
- ✅ `(* ... *)` — parenthesis-star-delimited comments

### **NOT SUPPORTED (Phase 2 and Beyond)**

- ❌ **Pointers/Dynamic Allocation**: No `^type`, `new`, or `dispose` keywords yet
- ❌ **Variant Records**: No discriminated union types
- ❌ **Enumerated Types**: No explicit `enum` keyword (ordinal types only via subranges)
- ❌ **Subrange Types**: No `1..10` type definitions (only array bounds)
- ❌ **Set Types**: No `set of` declarations or set operations
- ❌ **File Types**: No file I/O
- ❌ **With Statements**: No implicit field access
- ❌ **Conformant Arrays**: No flexible array parameters
- ❌ **Packed Types**: No bit-packing
- ❌ **Procedural Parameters**: No function pointers
- ❌ **Goto/Labels**: No non-local jumps
- ❌ **Real/Floating Point**: Not supported (24-bit integer ISA)
- ❌ **String Variables**: No `var s: string;` (only string literals in expressions)
- ❌ **Variant Parts in Records**: `record case ... of` not implemented

### **KEY CONSTRAINTS FOR YOUR LEXER**

1. **String Length**: Maximum 256 characters (defined in `lexer.h`)
2. **Identifier Length**: Maximum 64 characters (`MAX_LEXEME`)
3. **Case-Insensitive**: All identifiers are lowercased by the lexer
4. **Comment Nesting**: Supports both `{}` and `(**)` styles, but no nesting of mixed styles
5. **Negative Numbers**: Handled in parser, not lexer (lexer sees `-` as separate operator token)
6. **Integer Range**: 24-bit signed (-8,388,608 to 8,388,607)
7. **No Floating Point**: Integer-only

### **TOKENS YOUR LEXER SHOULD RECOGNIZE**

The existing lexer in `compiler/src/lexer.h` already defines all 62 token types needed. For your lexer implementation, focus on:

- **Keywords** (48): program, var, const, begin, end, if, then, else, while, do, for, to, downto, repeat, until, case, of, procedure, function, forward, type, array, record, char, integer, boolean, write, writeln, read, readln, uses, div, mod, and, or, not, true, false
- **Symbols** (14): `:=`, `;`, `.`, `,`, `(`, `)`, `+`, `-`, `*`, `=`, `<>`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, `[`, `]`, `:`
- **Special** (2): `..` (range), `'...'` (string/char literals)
- **Literals**: identifiers, integers, strings, characters
- **Comments**: `{...}` and `(*...*)`

### **RECOMMENDED IMPLEMENTATION NOTES**

1. The lexer is **case-insensitive** for keywords and identifiers (all lowercased)
2. Strings vs. characters distinguished by length: `'x'` is char, `'xy'` is string
3. No special escape sequences—strings are raw byte sequences
4. Comments don't nest across styles (can't nest `{...}` inside `(*...*)`)
5. Whitespace (space, tab, CR, LF) is skipped
6. The lexer produces a stream of tokens consumed by the recursive-descent parser

content.text: Now I need a script to run the lexer test. It needs to compile the test_lexer.pas, then run it with OCaml source as input.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# run-lexer-test.sh -- Compile and run the lexer test with OCaml source input
#
# Usage: ./scripts/run-lexer-test.sh <file.ml>
set -euo pipefail

ML="${1:?Usage: $0 <file.ml>}"
MAX_INSTRS="${2:-50000000}"

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

# Load vendored tool versions
. "$REPO_ROOT/vendor/active.env"

# Tool paths
P24P_S="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pascal/$SW_PASCAL_VERSION/bin/p24p.s"
RUNTIME="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pascal/$SW_PASCAL_VERSION/bin/runtime.spc"
PVM="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pvm.s"
PL24R="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pl24r"
PA24R="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pa24r"

# cor24-run: prefer vendored, fall back to system
COR24_RUN="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-em24/$SW_EM24_VERSION/bin/cor24-run"
if [ ! -f "$COR24_RUN" ]; then
    COR24_RUN="$(command -v cor24-run 2>/dev/null || true)"
fi
if [ -z "$COR24_RUN" ]; then
    echo "error: cor24-run not found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

LEXER_PAS="$REPO_ROOT/tests/test_lexer.pas"
TMP="/tmp/p24p_lextest_$$"
mkdir -p "$TMP"
trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT

# Resolve code_ptr address
CODE_PTR_ADDR=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$PVM" -e code_ptr --speed 0 -n 0 2>&1 | \
  grep "Entry point:" | sed 's/.*@ //')
if [ -z "$CODE_PTR_ADDR" ]; then
  echo "Error: could not resolve code_ptr" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Step 1: Compile test_lexer.pas to .spc
SPC_OUTPUT=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$P24P_S" -u "$(cat "$LEXER_PAS")"$'\x04' --speed 0 -n "$MAX_INSTRS" 2>&1 | \
  grep -v '^\[UART' | sed 's/^UART output: //')

if ! echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | grep -q "; OK"; then
  echo "Pascal compilation failed:" >&2
  echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | sed -n '/^\.module/,/^\.endmodule/p' > "$TMP/test_lexer.spc"

# Step 2: Link with runtime
"$PL24R" "$RUNTIME" "$TMP/test_lexer.spc" -o "$TMP/test_lexer_linked.spc" 2>/dev/null

# Step 3: Assemble
"$PA24R" "$TMP/test_lexer_linked.spc" -o "$TMP/test_lexer.p24" 2>/dev/null

# Step 4: Relocate
python3 "$SCRIPT_DIR/relocate_p24.py" "$TMP/test_lexer.p24" 0x010000 >/dev/null

# Step 5: Prepare input -- the OCaml source text
ML_INPUT=$(cat "$ML")

# Step 6: Run on PVM with OCaml source as UART input
printf '\x00\x00\x01' > "$TMP/code_ptr.bin"
"$COR24_RUN" --run "$PVM" \
  --load-binary "$TMP/test_lexer.bin@0x010000" \
  --load-binary "$TMP/code_ptr.bin@${CODE_PTR_ADDR}" \
  -u "${ML_INPUT}"$'\x04' --speed 0 -n "$MAX_INSTRS" 2>&1 | \
  grep -v '^\[' | grep -v '^Assembled' | grep -v '^Running' | \
  grep -v '^Executed' | grep -v '^Loaded' | grep -v '^PVM OK' | \
  grep -v '^$' | grep -v '^HALT$'

`exit` is not supported. I need to restructure the lexer to avoid `exit` -- use flag variables or restructure into nested if/else. Let me also look at the specific compilation error around array assignment:

The error at "expected END, got ASSIGN" around the `tok_id[tok_id_len] := src[pos]` line -- this might be an issue with how p24p handles assignment to `array[variable_expr]` inside a begin/end block. Let me look at how `is_digit(src[pos])` works.

Both work. So the issue is likely with `exit` and possibly the structure of the code. Let me look at the `.spc` output error more carefully. The error was: `error line 224: unknown procedure 'exit'` and then `error line 247: expected END, got ASSIGN`.

The second error is a cascade from the first -- once `exit` fails, the parser gets confused. Let me rewrite the lexer without `exit`, using a different control flow structure.

content.text:
The debug shows the real problem: `read(ch)` where `ch` is `char` is calling `_p24p_read_int` (reads parsed integers, not raw characters). Input "2 + 3" produces ordinals `2, 0, 3` instead of `50, 32, 43, 32, 51`.

The user wants me to file issues rather than work around compiler limits. Let me create GH issues on the Pascal compiler repo.

input.command: gh repo view sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner 2>/dev/null || echo "checking..."

content.content: sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal

toolUseResult.stdout: sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal

input.command:
gh issue create --repo sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal \
  --title "read(ch) for char variables calls _p24p_read_int instead of reading raw character" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

When `read(ch)` is called where `ch` is declared as `char`, the compiler generates a call to `_p24p_read_int` instead of a character-reading routine. This means the UART input is parsed as an integer rather than read as a raw character byte.

## Reproduction

```pascal
program TestRead;
var ch: char;
begin
  read(ch);
  writeln(ord(ch))
end.
```

With UART input `"A"` (ASCII 65), the expected output is `65`. Instead, the read fails or returns 0 because `_p24p_read_int` tries to parse `"A"` as a decimal number.

With UART input `"2 + 3"`, `read(ch)` returns integer `2` (not ASCII `50`), consuming the digits as a number. Subsequent reads return `0` (for `+`) and `3`.

## Impact

This blocks any program that needs to process raw text input character-by-character, which is essential for:
- Lexers / tokenizers
- Text processing programs
- Interactive character-oriented I/O

## Expected Fix

The compiler should generate a `_p24p_read_char` call (or equivalent) when the target variable is of type `char`. This routine should read exactly one byte from UART input and store its ordinal value, without any integer parsing.

## Location

- Compiler code generation: `compiler/src/codegen.c` (or wherever `read()` is lowered)
- Runtime: needs a new `_p24p_read_char` primitive in `runtime/runtime.spc`

## Context

Discovered while building an OCaml interpreter in Pascal (sw-cor24-ocaml). The interpreter needs to read OCaml source text character-by-character.
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh issue create --repo sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal \
  --title "Raise MAX_STRINGS limit from 16 to support larger programs" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

The string literal table is limited to 16 entries (`MAX_STRINGS = 16` in `compiler/src/parser.h`). Programs with more than 16 string literals fail with:

```
error: too many string literals (got STR_LIT)
```

## Impact

This limit is very restrictive for real programs. Even a simple test harness that prints token names like:

```pascal
if tok = 0 then write('EOF')
else if tok = 1 then write('INT')
else if tok = 2 then write('IDENT')
...
```

hits the limit at 17 string literals. Any program with moderate diagnostic output, error messages, or multi-string I/O will hit this.

## Suggested Fix

Raise `MAX_STRINGS` to at least 64 or 128 in `compiler/src/parser.h`:

```c
#define MAX_STRINGS 64
#define MAX_STRING_BYTES 256
```

The string table is statically allocated (`MAX_STRINGS * MAX_STRING_BYTES` = currently 4KB, would be 16KB at 64). This is well within COR24's memory budget.

## Location

- `compiler/src/parser.h:40` — `#define MAX_STRINGS 16`

## Context

Discovered while building an OCaml interpreter test harness in Pascal (sw-cor24-ocaml).
EOF
)"

  --title "Add exit procedure support for early return from procedures/functions" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

Standard Pascal's `exit` procedure (early return from the current procedure/function) is not recognized by p24p:

```
error: unknown procedure 'exit'
```

## Impact

Without `exit`, complex procedures must use deeply nested `if/else` chains or boolean flag variables to simulate early return. This makes code harder to read and maintain.

Example — a lexer's `lex_next` procedure naturally wants to return early after identifying each token type:

```pascal
procedure lex_next;
begin
  if is_digit(c) then begin
    { ... parse integer ... }
    exit  { <-- would return here }
  end;
  if is_alpha(c) then begin
    { ... parse identifier ... }
    exit
  end;
  { ... operators ... }
end;
```

Without `exit`, this requires a `done: boolean` flag and wrapping each branch in `if not done then`.

## Suggested Implementation

`exit` should generate a `ret` instruction, jumping to the procedure's epilogue. This is a common extension supported by Turbo Pascal, Free Pascal, and most modern Pascal implementations.

## Context

Discovered while building an OCaml interpreter in Pascal (sw-cor24-ocaml).
EOF
)"

content.text:
Now to answer your question about pointers:

**Yes, Pascal historically supports pointers.** Standard Pascal (ISO 7185) includes pointer types (`^T`), `new`, and `dispose` as core language features. They've been part of Pascal since Wirth's original design. Turbo Pascal, Free Pascal, and essentially every Pascal implementation supports them.

The array-indexing approach in the lexer is **not just a workaround** -- for a lexer specifically, flat arrays are a perfectly valid design (C lexers typically do the same). However, for the **parser and evaluator** (AST nodes, environments, closures), pointers and dynamic allocation are essential. Without them, building recursive tree structures is extremely awkward or impossible.

**The compiler should be updated.** The three issues filed are:

- sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal#4 -- `read(char)` reads integers, not raw chars (critical blocker)
- sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal#5 -- `MAX_STRINGS=16` too restrictive for real programs
- sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal#6 -- `exit` procedure not supported

A fourth issue for pointer support (`^T`, `new`, `dispose`) should be filed when the parser/evaluator step needs it -- that's the next blocker after these three.

message.content: add the issue for pointer feature

input.command:
gh issue create --repo sw-embed/sw-cor24-pascal \
  --title "Add pointer types, new, and dispose for dynamic allocation" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

p24p does not support pointer types (`^T`), `new`, or `dispose`. These are core Standard Pascal (ISO 7185) features present since Wirth's original language design and supported by every major Pascal implementation (Turbo Pascal, Free Pascal, Delphi, GNU Pascal).

## Impact

Without pointers and dynamic allocation, programs cannot build recursive data structures:

- **Linked lists** (environments, symbol tables)
- **Trees** (ASTs, expression trees)
- **Closures** (function values capturing their environment)

These are essential for any non-trivial program, especially compilers and interpreters.

## Required Features

### Pointer type declarations

```pascal
type
  PNode = ^Node;
  Node = record
    kind: integer;
    value: integer;
    left: PNode;
    right: PNode
  end;
```

Note: Standard Pascal allows forward-referencing the pointed-to type (`^Node` before `Node` is defined). This is necessary for recursive types.

### new — allocate heap memory

```pascal
var p: PNode;
begin
  new(p);
  p^.kind := 1;
  p^.value := 42;
  p^.left := nil;
  p^.right := nil
end.
```

### dispose — free heap memory

```pascal
dispose(p);
```

### nil — null pointer constant

```pascal
if p <> nil then
  writeln(p^.value)
```

### Pointer dereferencing with `^`

```pascal
p^.field     { access field of pointed-to record }
p^           { dereference to get the value }
```

## Implementation Notes

### Runtime

The P-code VM runtime already has `heap.pas` with heap management infrastructure. The runtime needs:
- A `_p24p_new(size)` primitive that allocates `size` words from the heap and returns the address
- A `_p24p_dispose(addr)` primitive that frees the block (can be a no-op initially)
- `nil` represented as address 0

### Compiler

- Parse `^TypeName` as a pointer type
- Parse `p^` and `p^.field` as dereference expressions
- Generate `call _p24p_new` for `new(p)` with the size of the pointed-to type
- Generate indirect load/store instructions for dereferenced access
- Support `nil` as a built-in constant (value 0)
- Allow forward type references for `^T` where `T` is defined later in the same `type` block

### P-code VM

May need indirect addressing instructions if not already present:
- Load word at address on stack (`loadi` / indirect load)
- Store word to address on stack (`storei` / indirect store)

## Context

Required by sw-cor24-ocaml to build AST nodes, environment chains, and closure values for the OCaml interpreter. Without this, the interpreter cannot represent parsed programs or runtime values.
EOF
)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# run-lexer-test.sh -- Compile and run the lexer test with OCaml source input
#
# Usage: ./scripts/run-lexer-test.sh <file.ml>
set -euo pipefail

ML="${1:?Usage: $0 <file.ml>}"
MAX_INSTRS="${2:-50000000}"

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

# Load vendored tool versions
. "$REPO_ROOT/vendor/active.env"

# Tool paths
P24P_S="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pascal/$SW_PASCAL_VERSION/bin/p24p.s"
RUNTIME="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pascal/$SW_PASCAL_VERSION/bin/runtime.spc"
PVM="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pvm.s"
PL24R="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pl24r"
PA24R="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-pcode/$SW_PCODE_VERSION/bin/pa24r"

# cor24-run: prefer vendored, fall back to system
COR24_RUN="$REPO_ROOT/vendor/sw-em24/$SW_EM24_VERSION/bin/cor24-run"
if [ ! -f "$COR24_RUN" ]; then
    COR24_RUN="$(command -v cor24-run 2>/dev/null || true)"
fi
if [ -z "$COR24_RUN" ]; then
    echo "error: cor24-run not found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

LEXER_PAS="$REPO_ROOT/tests/test_lexer.pas"
TMP="/tmp/p24p_lextest_$$"
mkdir -p "$TMP"
trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT

# Resolve code_ptr address
CODE_PTR_ADDR=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$PVM" -e code_ptr --speed 0 -n 0 2>&1 | \
  grep "Entry point:" | sed 's/.*@ //')
if [ -z "$CODE_PTR_ADDR" ]; then
  echo "Error: could not resolve code_ptr" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Step 1: Compile test_lexer.pas to .spc
SPC_OUTPUT=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$P24P_S" -u "$(cat "$LEXER_PAS")"$'\x04' --speed 0 -n "$MAX_INSTRS" 2>&1 | \
  grep -v '^\[UART' | sed 's/^UART output: //')

if ! echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | grep -q "; OK"; then
  echo "Pascal compilation failed:" >&2
  echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$SPC_OUTPUT" | sed -n '/^\.module/,/^\.endmodule/p' > "$TMP/test_lexer.spc"

# Step 2: Link with runtime
"$PL24R" "$RUNTIME" "$TMP/test_lexer.spc" -o "$TMP/test_lexer_linked.spc" 2>/dev/null

# Step 3: Assemble
"$PA24R" "$TMP/test_lexer_linked.spc" -o "$TMP/test_lexer.p24" 2>/dev/null

# Step 4: Relocate
python3 "$SCRIPT_DIR/relocate_p24.py" "$TMP/test_lexer.p24" 0x010000 >/dev/null

# Step 5: Prepare input -- the OCaml source text
ML_INPUT=$(cat "$ML")

# Step 6: Run on PVM with OCaml source as UART input
printf '\x00\x00\x01' > "$TMP/code_ptr.bin"
"$COR24_RUN" --run "$PVM" \
  --load-binary "$TMP/test_lexer.bin@0x010000" \
  --load-binary "$TMP/code_ptr.bin@${CODE_PTR_ADDR}" \
  -u "${ML_INPUT}"$'\x04' --speed 0 -n "$MAX_INSTRS" 2>&1 | \
  grep -v '^\[' | grep -v '^Assembled' | grep -v '^Running' | \
  grep -v '^Executed' | grep -v '^Loaded' | grep -v '^PVM OK' | \
  grep -v '^$' | grep -v '^HALT$'

feat(lexer): working OCaml tokenizer with regression tests

OCaml lexer (oc_lex.pas) tokenizes the full Phase 0 subset:
- Integer literals, identifiers, 11 keywords
- Operators: +, -, *, /, =, <>, <, >, <=, >=, &&, ||, ->
- Delimiters: (, ), ;
- Whitespace skipping and nested (* *) comment support
- Uses exit for clean early returns (p24p #6 fix)
- Uses string literals for token names (p24p #5 fix)
- Reads raw characters from input (p24p #4 fix)

Bump vendored Pascal compiler to 73e34aa (includes all 3 fixes).

Test harness (test_lexer.pas) prints token stream for any OCaml input.
4 reg-rs regression tests covering arithmetic, let bindings,
functions with arrow, and full factorial with comments.
All 6 tests pass (2 Pascal smoke + 4 lexer).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
